coredata.dc:title: Developing oxygen vacancy-rich CuMn2O4/carbon dots dual-function nanozymes via Chan-Lam coupling reaction for the colorimetric/fluorescent determination of D-penicillamine
coredata.prism:copyright: © 2024 Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.prism:publisher: Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.
coredata.dc:creator: Feng, Min
Zhang, Xiaodan
Huang, Yuming
coredata.dc:description: Defect engineering is a promising approach to construct high performance nanozymes due to its ability to regulate their physical and chemical properties. However, how to construct defects to improve the activity of nanozymes remains a challenge. Herein, for the first time, the Chan-Lam coupling reaction is used to construct the oxygen vacancy (OV)-rich CuMn2O4/carbon dots (CDs) (OV-CuMn2O4/CDs) dual-function nanozymes with fluorescent (FL) and oxidase-like properties, via regulating the low-valent metal ions (Cu+ and Mn2+) and Ov contents in the spinel CuMn2O4 and in-situ growth of β-cyclodextrin (β-CD)-derived CDs. Expectedly, relative to CuMn2O4, the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs exhibited 35.8%, 8.5%, and 14.6% rise in the contents of Cu+, Mn2+ and Ov, respectively. Abundant Ov provides more O2 adsorption/activation sites, and the charge transfer between Ov and metal atoms increases the charge density around metal atoms. This produces more low-valent metals (like Cu+ and Mn2+) to promote the electron transfer from metal to O atoms and O-O bond cleavage. Thus, the oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs is 4.1 times that of CuMn2O4. Also, the in-situ growth of β-CD-derived carbon dots on CuMn2O4 endows OV-CuMn2O4/CDs selective target recognition. Thus, a sensitive and selective colorimetric and fluorescence dual-mode method was established for determining D-penicillamine (D-PA), with the limit of detection of 0.25 and 0.048 μM, respectively. The method was applied to D-PA determination in real samples. This work demonstrates the Chan-Lam coupling reaction can be used to construct high performance nanozymes for developing dual-mode sensor for the detection of targets.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Chan-Lam coupling reaction
Defect engineering
Multifunctional nanozyme
Dual-mode sensing
D-penicillamine

xocs:item-toc-entry.xocs:item-toc-section-title: Oxidase-like activity and fluorescent property evaluation
Colorimetric and fluorescent dual-mode detection of D-PA
Analysis of D-PA in the real sample
The oxidase-like and fluorescent properties of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs
Steady-state kinetic assay and catalytic mechanism for OV-CuMn2O4/CDs
Performance of the dual-mode platform for D-PA detection

xocs:cp-notices.xocs:cp-notice: © 2024 Elsevier B.V. All rights are reserved, including those for text and data mining, AI training, and similar technologies.

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: (a) Schematic of preparation of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs. (b) SEM, (c) TEM, (d–e) HRTEM and (f) element mapping profiles of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs.

(a) Michaelis–Menten curves and (b) the corresponding double-reciprocal plots for CuMn2O4 and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs with TMB as substrates. (c) CV curves and (d) EIS Nyquist plots of CuMn2O4 and Ov-CuMn2O4/CDs. Inset in Fig. 2d shows the equivalent circuit. (e) UV–vis spectrum of oxTMB and fluorescence spectra of Ov-CuMn2O4/CDs (excitation: 370 nm). (f) The excited state lifetime of Ov-CuMn2O4/CDs in the presence or absence of TMB.

Effects of (a) pH and (b) temperature on the oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs. Conditions: 10 mg/L catalyst, 0.1 mM TMB and 20 min of reaction time. The error bar represents the standard deviation of the three parallel trials. (c) Time scan curve of TMB + OV-CuMn2O4/CDs system with or without 50 μM D-PA. (d) UV–vis spectra of TMB + OV-CuMn2O4/CDs system after adding different concentrations of D-PA. (e) Linear plot of ΔA 652 versus D-PA concentration (ΔA 652 represents the change of A 652 before and after introducing D-PA.). (f) Fluorescent spectrum of TMB + OV-CuMn2O4/CDs system after adding different concentration of D-PA. (g) Linear relation between (F-F 0) and D-PA concentration (F 0 and F represent the fluorescence intensity at 420 nm of TMB/OV-CuMn2O4/CDs system before and after introducing D-PA.). (h–i) Effect of different substances on the absorbance of TMB/OV-CuMn2O4/CDs system at 652 nm (1 mM small molecules and ions, except D-PA and magnesium stearate at 50 μM, and cysteine and glutathione at 4 μM).
Detection results of D-PA in the tablets and water samples.

head.ce:title: Developing oxygen vacancy-rich CuMn2O4/carbon dots dual-function nanozymes via Chan-Lam coupling reaction for the colorimetric/fluorescent determination of D-penicillamine

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory of Luminescence Analysis and Molecular Sensing (Ministry of Education), College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Southwest University, Chongqing, 400715, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory of Luminescence Analysis and Molecular Sensing (Ministry of Education)
College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering

ce:affiliation.ce:source-text: Institute of Analytical Sciences, Southwest University, Chongqing, 400715, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Defect engineering is a promising approach to construct high performance nanozymes due to its ability to regulate their physical and chemical properties. However, how to construct defects to improve the activity of nanozymes remains a challenge. Herein, for the first time, the Chan-Lam coupling reaction is used to construct the oxygen vacancy (OV)-rich CuMn2O4/carbon dots (CDs) (OV-CuMn2O4/CDs) dual-function nanozymes with fluorescent (FL) and oxidase-like properties, via regulating the low-valent metal ions (Cu+ and Mn2+) and Ov contents in the spinel CuMn2O4 and in-situ growth of β-cyclodextrin (β-CD)-derived CDs. Expectedly, relative to CuMn2O4, the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs exhibited 35.8%, 8.5%, and 14.6% rise in the contents of Cu+, Mn2+ and Ov, respectively. Abundant Ov provides more O2 adsorption/activation sites, and the charge transfer between Ov and metal atoms increases the charge density around metal atoms. This produces more low-valent metals (like Cu+ and Mn2+) to promote the electron transfer from metal to O atoms and O-O bond cleavage. Thus, the oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs is 4.1 times that of CuMn2O4. Also, the in-situ growth of β-CD-derived carbon dots on CuMn2O4 endows OV-CuMn2O4/CDs selective target recognition. Thus, a sensitive and selective colorimetric and fluorescence dual-mode method was established for determining D-penicillamine (D-PA), with the limit of detection of 0.25 and 0.048 μM, respectively. The method was applied to D-PA determination in real samples. This work demonstrates the Chan-Lam coupling reaction can be used to construct high performance nanozymes for developing dual-mode sensor for the detection of targets.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: Data will be made available on request.

ce:section.ce:para: D-penicillamine (D-PA) is an S-consisting amino acid and often utilized as an important pharmaceutical for treating Wilson's disease, toxic metal poisoning, rheumatoid arthritis, chronic active hepatitis, and scleroderma. It has been listed as an essential drug in basic medical care by the World Health Organization (Agarwal et al., 2016; Liu et al., 2021; Zhou et al., 2024b). However, inappropriate intake of D-PA can cause side effects, such as mouth ulcers, aging, severe muscle weakness and dermatitis (Kumar et al., 2020). Also, excessive use can lead to environmental accumulation, posing a serious threat to human health and ecological environment (Ge et al., 2018). Thus, much progress has been made in developing analytical methods for the D-PA detection (Hou et al., 2022; Liu et al., 2023a; Ngamdee et al., 2015). However, most are based on single-signal detection mode. They are susceptible to potential interference, leading to false or inaccurate results. In addition, some methods can not realize the visual fast detection on site. Hence, it is urgent to develop a simple, convenient, visualized, and sensitive assay for the detection of D-PA.

The non-enzymatic biosensing assays received continuously growing interest during the last decades (Emir et al., 2021), in particular, the nanozymes-based colorimetric ones have been widely studied in the past years due to their low cost, simple operation and naked-eye observation (Liu et al., 2024). However, they are susceptible to the interference of complex environmental matrix color, resulting in false positive results (Li et al., 2022). In addition, the activity of nanozymes significantly affects the sensitivity of the detection method (Wu et al., 2022a). Hence, the rational design of high activity nanozymes for the construction of colorimetric/fluorescent dual-mode sensing platform can not only inherit the advantages of colorimetry, but also promise the high sensitivity of fluorescence method, which can effectively meet the current needs of D-PA determination. More importantly, the results of the two modes can be verified with each other to ensure the accuracy of the results (Chen et al., 2022).

Since Fe3O4 was found to hold peroxidase-like activity (Gao et al., 2007), researchers have been working on developing highly effective and unique nanozymes (Fan et al., 2024). Particularly, spinel oxide is one of ideal candidates for the design of high performance nanozymes because of its adjustable composition, special d-orbital filling, synergy between the components and high conductivity (Feng et al., 2024; Zhou et al., 2022). Unfortunately, many published spinel nanozymes promise low activity. Further, their catalytic activity depends on the metal sites exposed on the surface, and there is no specific recognition pocket. Therefore, improving the activity of spinel nanozymes and constructing selective recognition pockets have become research hotspots.

Defect engineering is considered to be an effective method to enhance spinel nanozyme activity and selectivity (Wu et al., 2022b). The construction of defects can expose a large number of unsaturated sites, virtually propel ion spread and charge movement, and offer extra adsorption positions for guest molecules and intermediate substances (Zhang et al., 2020). In addition, the presence of defects will disturb surrounding atoms and cause lattice distortion, which can effectively adjust the electronic structure, chemical properties and conductivity of the material, further affecting its enzymatic activity (Wu et al., 2022b). The existence of oxygen vacancies (OV) can appropriately regulate the metal ion valence state of transition metal oxides and affect the catalytic performance (Zhang et al., 2020). At present, the main methods for constructing defects include strong acid/alkali etching, chemical reagent reduction, sonication and laser treatment (Ji et al., 2023; Muhammad et al., 2024; Sun et al., 2023a). These methods often have some problems, such as strong corrosive reagent, severe reaction and harsh reaction conditions. The Chan-Lam coupling reaction has mild reaction conditions and does not require strong acid/base or strong reducing agent (Han et al., 2018; Walker et al., 2021; West et al., 2019). In the reaction process, boric acid is complexed with the catalyst, and then, the metal ions are irreversibly transferred, and the divalent copper complex is oxidized to trivalent copper complex, which is reduced and eliminated by reaction with amine to obtain the product (Scheme S1). Therefore, the irreversible transfer of metals during the Chan-Lam coupling reaction may be a promising method to construct defects.

Luminous carbon dots (CDs) have been widely used in sensing because of their easy surface modification, strong photo-bleaching resistance, good biocompatibility and weak toxicity (Kagkoura et al., 2023; Li et al., 2023; Xu et al., 2023). Similar to its precursors, CDs possess high preference to consequential object through surface-modified functional groups, and are one of ideal materials for recognizing and detecting unique objective (Liang et al., 2024). β-Cyclodextrin (β-CD) is considered to be the ideal host molecule, and the geometric matching of its internal cavities and branched hydroxyl groups can provide a microenvironment for molecular recognition, and the external hydrophilic groups are conducive to improving the hydrophilicity and dispersion of the material (Durán et al., 2017; Hou et al., 2022). Thus, the selection of β-CD as a precursor to synthesize carbon dots can not only construct selective recognition pockets, but also improve hydrophilicity and enhance enzyme activity.

Herein, for the first time, the OV-rich CuMn2O4/CDs dual-function nanozyme was prepared by introducing rich OV in the CuMn2O4 via Chan-Lam coupling reaction, and using β-CD as precursor to in-situ load CDs. The elevated OV leads to a change in electronic structure, producing more low-valent metal ions and improving charge transfer efficiency. The presence of CDs gives the material fluorescent property and high hydrophilicity. As expected, compared with CuMn2O4, the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs showed significant oxidase-like activity and selective recognition of D-penicillamine. Based on the excellent oxidase-like activity and fluorescent property of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs, a colorimetric/fluorescence dual-mode sensing platform for the D-PA detection was constructed and applied to the determination of D-PA in commercial penicillamine pills and aqueous samples.

The method in the reference was modified to prepare CuMn2O4 (Zhu et al., 2021). In brief, 10 mmol of Cu(CH3COO)2·H2O, 20 mmol of Mn(CH3COO)2, 0.1 mol of urea, 6 mmol of KBrO3 and 16 mmol of NH4F were mixed with 200 mL of pure H2O and agitated for 30 min. Then, the mixture was transferred to an autoclave, which was heated to 160 °C and retained for 8 h. After the reaction, the solid was collected and washed three times with ultrapure water and ethanol, dried at 60 °C for 12 h, then calcined at 500 °C in air atmosphere for 4 h to obtain CuMn2O4.

The OV-CuMn2O4/CDs was synthesized via one-step hydro-thermal method. In detail, 0.5 g of phenylboronic acid, 0.6 g of β-CD and 200 mg of CuMn2O4 were dispersed into 25 mL of formamide, then the mixture was continuously stirred until the phenylboronic acid and β-CD were completely dissolved, then transferred to an autoclave and heated to 180 °C and kept for 4 h. After natural cooling to room temperature, the solids were collected, washed three times each with ultrapure water and ethanol, and dried at 60 °C. The OV-CuMn2O4 and CuMn2O4/CDs were synthesized by the same method as the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs without using β-CD and phenylboronic acid, respectively. CDs were prepared without using CuMn2O4 and phenylboronic acid under the same conditions as the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Oxidase-like activity and fluorescent property evaluation
ce:section.ce:para: The oxidase-like activity was studied using 3,3′,5,5′-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) as a chromogenic substrate. To do this, 2 mg of the obtained material was sonicated and dispersed into 4 mL of ultrapure water to obtain a 500 μg mL−1 dispersion. Then, 0.1 mL of dispersion solution and 0.25 mL of TMB (2 mM) were mixed with 4.65 mL of NaAc-HAc buffer (pH=3.5). After 20 min reaction at 25 °C, the UV–vis spectra of the solution and the absorbance at 652 nm (A 652) were recorded.
To study the fluorescent property of the material, 0.1 mL of material dispersion in water was added to 4.9 mL of NaAc-HAc buffer (pH=3.5), and the fluorescent spectra were recorded.

Steady-state kinetic analysis was performed in NaAc-HAc buffer containing 10 mg/L OV-CuMn2O4/CDs and different concentrations of TMB. Accordingly, different concentrations of TMB (0.01–0.3 mM) were added to NaAc-HAc buffer (pH=3.5) with 10 mg/L OV-CuMn2O4/CDs, and the time-scan curves were recorded. The kinetic parameters of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs were determined by equation V = (V max × [S])/(K m + [S]) (Sun et al., 2023b), here, V and V max are the initial and the maximum reaction rates, respectively, [S] denotes the substrate concentration, and K m is Michaelis constant. The kinetic parameters of CuMn2O4 were obtained by the same method.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Colorimetric and fluorescent dual-mode detection of D-PA
ce:section.ce:para: Under optimal conditions, 50 μL of D-PA solution with different concentrations (5 − 9000 μM), 0.1 mL of 500 mg/L OV-CuMn2O4/CDs and 0.25 mL of 2 mM TMB were added to 4.60 mL of the NaAc-HAc buffer, followed by incubation at 25 °C for 20 min. Then, the colorimetric and fluorescent signals were recorded separately. The fluorescent trial was performed at excitation/emission wavelengths of 370 nm/420 nm. The excitation/emission slits were set at 10 nm, and PMT voltage was 700 V.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analysis of D-PA in the real sample
ce:section.ce:para: D-PA contents in actual water samples and tablets were analyzed by the proposed method to verify its feasibility. The water samples were collected from Jialing River and filtered with 0.22 μM filter membrane for future use. For the detection of D-PA in tablets, the D-PA tablets were from local drug store, and thoroughly ground into a powder and dissolved in 100 mL of ultrapure water, followed by the filtration using a 0.22 μM membrane. The filtrate was diluted 10-fold with pure water for further use. Meanwhile, the D-PA recovery rate tests were carried out, in which the spiked samples with different levels of D-PA (1, 20 and 40 μM) were used. The concentration of D-PA was measured as described above.

As illustrated in Fig. 1 a, the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs was prepared by etching CuMn2O4 and in-situ growing carbon dots through Chan-Lam coupling reaction. The effect of CuMn2O4 dosage on the oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs was investigated (Fig. S1). The OV-CuMn2O4/CDs prepared by using 200 mg CuMn2O4 had the best oxidase-like activity. This may be due to the structural collapse of CuMn2O4 due to etching at low concentrations, while the ideal etching effect cannot be achieved at high concentrations. The SEM (Fig. S2) and TEM (Fig. S3) images show that the CuMn2O4 displays an octahedral cone structure with a width of 3 μm and a length of 5 μm, and its surface is relatively smooth. The OV-CuMn2O4/CDs maintains the octahedral cone of CuMn2O4, but its volume is reduced by about 1/3 and its surface becomes rough (Fig. 1b). As shown in Fig. 1c, the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs exhibits a hollow structure with a rough surface. This is because during the reaction, phenylboronic acid complexes with Cu2+ in the CuMn2O4 to promote metal transfer (Scheme S1). This process is irreversible, leading to the etching of CuMn2O4 and the appearance of a hollow structure and rough surface. Therefore, the Chan-Lam coupling reaction can be used to etch Cu2+-containing materials in a controlled manner to achieve the desired effect. Fig. 1d confirms the successful loading of CDs on CuMn2O4, some of which are marked with red circles for clear observation. The high-resolution transmission electron microscopy (HRTEM) photo reveals two lattice fringe spacings of 0.256 and 0.483 nm (Fig. 1e), corresponding to the (311) and (101) crystal faces of CuMn2O4 (Zhou et al., 2022; Zhu et al., 2021). The element mapping photos show that the O, Mn and Cu were uniformly dispersed in CuMn2O4 (Fig. S4) and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs (Fig. 1f). Compared with CuMn2O4, the Cu content in OV-CuMn2O4/CDs is significantly reduced, further confirming that Chan-Lam caused irreversible transfer of metals. To demonstrate the successful loading of CDs, the Zeta potentials of CDs, CuMn2O4 and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs were analyzed. Fig. S5 shows that they are 15.52 mV and −5.55 mV for the CDs and CuMn2O4, respectively, while it is −1.49 mV for the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs. This implies the successful combination of CDs and CuMn2O4. As shown in Fig. S6, XRD results show that the CuMn2O4 and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs exhibit a good crystal pattern and consistent with the standard card of tetragonal CuMn2O4 (JCPDS no.34-1322), confirming the existence of a high crystalline tetragonal phase (Zhou et al., 2022).

XPS was conducted to elucidate the elemental constitutions and chemical valence status of CuMn2O4 and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs. Fig. S7a shows that the Cu, Mn, O and C elements exist in the CuMn2O4 and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs. In addition, peaks of N 1s and B 1s are found in OV-CuMn2O4/CDs, where B element is from phenylboronic acid and N element is from formamide. The O 1s spectrum consists of three peaks matching the lattice-oxygen (OL), oxygen-vacancies (OV), and surface adsorbed oxygen (OA) (Fig. S7b). Interestingly, compared with CuMn2O4, the OL content in OV-CuMn2O4/CDs is significantly reduced, while the OV and OA contents increase by 14.6% and 22.1% (Table S1), respectively. The emergence of oxygen vacancy in nanozymes is beneficial for the capture of oxygen-containing compounds and the improvement of oxidase-like activity (Zhao et al., 2021). Furthermore, oxygen vacancy is a type of defects able to render extra active positions to catch more O2 to produce chemisorption of O2, and is also considered an active site that can catalyze O2 to produce superoxide radicals (O2 •−) (Liu et al., 2023b). Fig. S7c shows that the Cu 2p spectrum is deconvoluted to two major peaks, belonging to Cu 2p3/2 and Cu 2p1/2. The peaks at about 932 and 952 eV belong to Cu+, and the other peaks at ∼934 and ∼954 eV owe to Cu2+ (Wang et al., 2023). After the Chan-Lam coupling reaction, due to the increase of OV in the material and the increase of charge density around the Cu core, the Cu 2p peak shifted to the lower energy by ∼1.58 eV (Zheng et al., 2024). Moreover, the Cu+ content increased from 18.8% to 54.6% (Table S1). The XPS spectra of Mn 2p for CuMn2O4 and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs are shown in Fig. S7d. The results showed that both materials contained Mn2+, Mn3+ and Mn4+. Compared with CuMn2O4, the contents of Mn3+ and Mn4+ in OV-CuMn2O4/CDs decreased, while that of Mn2+ increased by 8.5% (Table S1). In general, the occurrence of Chan-Lam coupling reaction not only causes the metal to be irreversibly transferred to form a rough surface and hollow structure, but also leads to a significant increase in the content of OV and low metal in the material. OV can adsorb O2, and low-valent metal ions will give electrons to promote O-O cleavage or direct transfer to TMB to improve the oxidase-like activity of nanozymes.

The structural properties of CuMn2O4 and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs were studied by nitrogen adsorption-desorption trial, and the sequences were displayed in Fig. S8. Both two samples exhibit typical type IV characteristic curves, exhibiting an H3 lag loops. This implies that both CuMn2O4 and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs have irregular meso-pores. The H3 hysteresis loops with P/P 0 between 0.8 and 1 showed that there were lamellar aggregation and slit pores in the two samples, and there were more pores in OV-CuMn2O4/CDs (Li et al., 2024). From Table S2, the surface area (S BET) and pore volume (V P) for CuMn2O4 were only 3.92 m2 g−1 and 0.026 cm3 g−1. However, the S BET and V P of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs were 9.00 m2 g−1 and 0.034 cm3 g−1. The results show that the Chan-Lam coupling reaction can improve the S BET and V P of the material, which is consistent with the SEM and TEM results. Therefore, OV-CuMn2O4/CDs with high S BET and V P is more conducive to the exposure of active sites and substrate transport, and has superior oxidase-like activity (Lu et al., 2022).

ce:section.ce:section-title: The oxidase-like and fluorescent properties of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs
ce:section.ce:para: To study the oxidase-like property of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs, TMB was chosen as the chromogenic substrate. As depicted in Fig. S9, the reaction system contains OV-CuMn2O4/CDs and TMB, the colorless TMB will be oxidized to form blue oxTMB, and a characteristic absorption peak appears at 652 nm in the UV–vis absorption curve. However, TMB oxidation was inhibited after the introduction of H2O2, suggesting that H2O2 reduced the oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs. Although MnO2 has been reported to have oxidase-like activity (Zhang et al., 2024; Dong et al., 2023), it does not react directly with H2O2 and only acts as a catalyst to promote its decomposition. Thus, this may be because the oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs is highly dependent on Cu+. After the addition of H2O2, Cu+ is oxidized to Cu2+, leading to the inhibition of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs’ activity. To confirm the conversion of Cu+ to Cu2+, we performed XPS analysis of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs after reaction with H2O2. As shown in Fig. S10, after reaction with H2O2, the Cu+ content in the treated OV-CuMn2O4/CDs decreased from 54.6% to 36.6%, while the Cu2+ content increased from 45.4% to 63.4%. This implies that Cu+ is oxidized to Cu2+. Above results indicated the specific oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs.

In addition, the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs was added to the buffer solution consisting of OPD (o-phenylenediamine) or ABTS (2,2'-azino-bis(3-ethylbenzo-thiazoline-6-sulfonic acid) diammonium salt), and the color of the solution varied from colorless to yellow or green, respectively, and the characteristic absorption peaks of oxOPD and oxABTS appeared in their UV–Vis absorption curves, which further confirmed that OV-CuMn2O4/CDs had oxidase-like activity (Fig. S11). In addition, the oxidase-like activities of the five materials were compared. As shown in Fig. S12, CDs has almost no oxidase-like activity, and the oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs was improved by about 4.1 times (see calculation details in Supporting Information) compared to CuMn2O4. Among them, the occurrence of Chan-Lam coupling reaction makes the material produce rough surface and porous structure, which is conducive to the exposure of more active sites and the transport of substrates. Moreover, the content of low metal and OV increased significantly after the reaction, which was conducive to the enzymatic reaction. The oxidase-like activity of CuMn2O4 can be significantly enhanced through the Chan-Lam coupling reaction. In addition, the synergistic effect of CDs with CuMn2O4 and the abundant organic groups on its surface can improve the hydrophilicity and enhance the activity of nanozymes. Improving the hydrophilicity of nanozymes can make them have better water dispersibility and promote the contact of the active sites with the substrate in solution (Jiao et al., 2020). In order to prove that CDs loading can improve the hydrophilicity of CuMn2O4, contact angle determination was further fulfilled. As disclosed in Fig. S13, after the introduction of CDs, the contact angle decreases from 38.7° to 23.2°, indicating that its hydrophilicity is significantly improved.

The stability of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs was evaluated. The oxidase-like activities of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs synthesized in five different batches were analyzed, and the relative standard deviation (RSD) < 5% (Fig. S14a), demonstrating good reproducibility. After 30 days of storage at room temperature, the oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs did not change significantly, which proved its good long-term storage stability (Fig. S14b). After treatment at different temperatures (Fig. S15a) and pH (Fig. S15b) for 2 h, the oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs remained above 85%, indicating that it had good temperature and pH tolerance.

The CDs were synthesized based on previous reports, and their optimal excitation wavelength was reported to be 370 nm. Therefore, the fluorescence spectra were obtained at an excitation wavelength of 370 nm. As shown in Fig. S16a, the CuMn2O4 does not exhibit fluorescent emission, while the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs exhibit obvious fluorescent emission, which is similar to that of CDs. In addition, the fluorescence response of the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs solution increased with its concentration (Fig. S16b). This implies that the introduction of CDs confers fluorescence properties of CuMn2O4, which further confirms the successful synthesis of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs. This provides a basis for constructing a colorimetric/fluorescence dual-mode detection method.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Steady-state kinetic assay and catalytic mechanism for OV-CuMn2O4/CDs
ce:section.ce:para: Using TMB as chromogenic substrate, the steady-state kinetic trial was performed to evaluate the catalytic efficacy of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs. The K m and V max were acquired according to the Michaelis-Menten (Fig. 2 a) and Lineweaver-Burk plots (Fig. 2b). The K m value can be used to assess preference between the enzyme and the substrate, and the lower the K m, the higher the affinity (Ye et al., 2023). The concentration of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs was 10 mg/L, its K m and V max were 0.05 mM and 9.36 × 10−8 M/s, respectively. In addition, according to the Michaelis-Menten curve (Fig. 2a) and corresponding Lineweaver-Burk plot (Fig. 2b) of CuMn2O4, K m and V max were 0.010 mM and 0.98 × 10−8 M/s, respectively. The V max of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs is 9.55 times larger than that of CuMn2O4, indicating that OV-CuMn2O4/CDs has higher enzyme-like catalytic efficiency. Compared with other spinel oxide nanozymes, OV-CuMn2O4/CDs has a relatively lower K m and a larger V max, indicating that it has better affinity for the substrate TMB and higher oxidase-like activity (Table S3).

The electrical conductivity of CuMn2O4 and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs was analyzed by cyclic voltammetry (CV) in 5 × 10−3 mol/L K3Fe[CN)6]/K4Fe[CN)6] solution with 100 mV s−1 scan rate in the −0.2 V ∼ +0.8 V voltage scope. Compared with CuMn2O4, the redox peaks of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs is obviously increased, which indicates that Ov-CuMn2O4/CDs has better electrical conductivity (Fig. 2c). In order to further study the electrochemical properties of CuMn2O4 and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs, electro-chemical impedance spectroscopy (EIS) analysis was carried out. The electrode reaction process consists of charge transfer and diffusion. There is a semicircle in the high frequency location, while it is a 45° straight line in the low frequency one. The former is closely related to the electron transfer process, and the diameter of semicircle reflects the charge transfer resistance, noted as Rct (Liang et al., 2023). According to Fig. 2d, relative to that of CuMn2O4 (R ct = 930.7 Ω), the R ct of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs (135.8 Ω) is significantly reduced, and it is smaller than those of Mn2O3 (R ct = 1257 Ω) and CuO (R ct = 6437 Ω) (Gao et al., 2021). This indicates that the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs has a better charge transfer efficiency, which is consistent with the CV test results. The excellent electrical conductivity and electron transfer efficiency of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs are mainly attributed to the abundance of OV and CDs.

In general, reactive oxygen species (ROS) and electron transfer determine the catalytic performance of nanozymes. Sodium azide (NaN3), p-benzoquinone (PBQ) and thiourea (TH) were used as scavengers of the 1O2, O2 •− and •OH, respectively, and the effect of Ov was explored by EDTA-2Na (Liu et al., 2020). The addition of NaN3 did not inhibit A 652. With the increase of PBQ, TH and EDTA-2Na concentrations, A 652 was significantly reduced, indicating that the production of O2 •− and •OH in the system and the presence of Ov in OV-CuMn2O4/CDs played an important role in the improvement of enzyme activity (Fig. S17a). To further confirm the presence of ROS in the reaction system and Ov in OV-CuMn2O4/CDs, electron paramagnetic resonance (EPR) analysis was performed. Using 5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline N-oxide (DMPO) as trapping reagent, •OH and O2 •− were analyzed in aqueous solution and methanol solution respectively. The signal peaks of •OH-DMPO (Fig. S17b) and O2 •−-DMPO (Fig. S17c) were clearly detected, which were significantly enhanced at 5 min compared with those at 2 min, confirming the formation of •OH and O2 •− during the reaction. Furthermore, the presence of Ov in OV-CuMn2O4/CDs was confirmed by the obvious EPR signal (Fig. S17d) (Lin et al., 2024). In addition, compared with the oxidation of TMB in air-saturated buffer solutions, the oxidation of TMB was significantly promoted or inhibited when the reaction was carried out in O2 or Ar-saturated buffer solutions (Fig. S18). It is proved that O2 plays an indispensable role in the oxidation process of TMB.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Performance of the dual-mode platform for D-PA detection
ce:section.ce:para: The factors affecting the oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs were explored to obtain the optimal reaction conditions. Fig. S19a shows that the A 652 gradually increased with the increase of reaction time and remained stable at 20 min. Notably, the fluorescence peak intensity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs decreased with the increase of time and remained basically unchanged at 20 min (Fig. S19b). Therefore, 20 min was chosen as the reaction time of enzyme-like catalysis in this work. The UV–Vis absorption spectrum of oxTMB overlap with the fluorescence emission spectra of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs (Fig. 2e). In addition, the fluorescence lifetimes of TMB + OV-CuMn2O4/CDs and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs were compared, and there was no significant difference (Fig. 2f). Therefore, the fluorescence quenching of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs is attributed to the internal filtration effect (IFE) (Wang et al., 2022). In other words, the fluorescence intensity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs decreases due to its absorption by oxTMB. In addition, pH and temperature effects on the oxidase-like activity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs were investigated. The results showed that OV-CuMn2O4/CDs had better oxidase-like activity when the pH = 3.5 and the incubation temperature was 35 °C (Fig. 3 a and b). In addition, the activity of OV-CuMn2O4 remained above 80% in a wide temperature range (15–45 °C) (Fig. 3b). Considering that there was no significant difference in activity between 25 °C and 35 °C, and the actual samples subsequently selected were drug and water samples, the incubation temperature was finally selected at 25 °C.

As a sulfhydryl-containing amino acid, D-PA has certain oxidant resistance and reducing properties, and it can bind to the pockets of β-CD through host-guest interaction (Durán et al., 2017; Hou et al., 2022). As shown in Fig. 3c, A 652 decreased sharply after D-PA was added, indicating that D-PA could reduce oxTMB (curves a and c). If D-PA is added before the start of the reaction, it will precede the ROS reaction and delay the oxidation of TMB (plot b). Interestingly, the three lines remained basically parallel in the later stage, indicating that D-PA did not directly react with OV-CuMn2O4/CDs to reduce its activity, but with oxTMB or ROS to inhibit the oxidation of TMB (Tao et al., 2024). Meanwhile, the fluorescence of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs gradually recovered after the addition of D-PA (Fig. S20). After the addition of D-PA, oxTMB formation decreased, resulting in a decrease in IFE and the fluorescence recovery of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs. Therefore, the simultaneous change of A 652 and fluorescence intensity of OV-CuMn2O4/CDs provides a possibility for the construction of D-PA colorimetric/fluorescence dual-mode sensing platform.

Under optimal conditions, a colorimetric detection method for D-PA was constructed firstly. As depicted in Fig. 3d, the A 652 of the TMB + OV-CuMn2O4/CDs system gradually decreases with D-PA concentration, accompanied by color fading (inset in Fig. 3d). There is a good linear relationship between A 652 and D-penicillamine concentration in the 0.25 – 90 μM range (Fig. 3e). The linear fitting equation is ΔA 652 = 0.012 [D-PA] + 0.035 (r 2 = 0.995, n = 14), and the limit of detection (LOD) is 250 nM (3σ) (Zhou et al., 2024a).

Second, a fluorescent detection method for D-PA was developed. Fig. 3f shows that the fluorescent signal of TMB + OV-CuMn2O4/CDs system increases with D-PA concentration. The maximal fluorescence signal at 420 nm (F 420 nm) showed a linear relation with D-penicillamine concentration in the 0.05 – 20 μM and 20 – 90 μM ranges (Fig. 3g), respectively. The corresponding fitting formulas are ΔF 420 = 15.93 [D-PA] − 1.81 and ΔF 420 nm = 45.06 [D-PA] − 615.9, respectively. The LOD is as low as 0.048 μM (3σ). Compared with other D-PA detection methods (Table S4), the proposed dual-mode detection strategy can obtain a lower LOD and a wider detection range. It is noteworthy that the colorimetry in our dual-mode detection strategy can be used for visual detection in the field, while the fluorescence method can avoid color interference in complex matrices and has high sensitivity for laboratory analysis. Thus, this strategy can be used for determining D-PA in different testing environments. In addition, the analysis results of the two modes can be mutually verified to ensure the reliability of the detection results. The selectivity of the TMB + OV-CuMn2O4/CDs system for D-PA was evaluated. The results showed that the addition of different small molecules and ions had little effect on A 652 (Fig. 3h–i) and F 420 nm (Fig. S21), indicating its good selectivity for determining D-penicillamine.

To demonstrate the analytical application of the colorimetric/fluorescence dual-mode sensing platform, the D-PA content in commercial D-PA pills and Jialing River waters were analyzed. According to the tablet instruction, the stated content of D-PA in each penicillamine tablet is 0.125 g. The D-PA content in commercial D-PA tablets measured by the proposed colorimetric/fluorescence dual-mode assay was 0.126 ± 0.001 and 0.127 ± 0.004 g/tablet, respectively, which was consistent with its stated one. In addition, the results showed that the content of D-PA in D-penicillamine tablets was 100.8% and 101.6%, respectively, which met the national standard (>95%) (The Pharmacopoeia Committee, 2020). As shown in Table 1 , the spiked recoveries were in the 93.6%–109.0% range with the RSD of ≤6.3%. This indicates that the technique may be utilized for the accurate determination of D-penicillamine in actual samples.

In conclusion, via Chan-Lam coupling reaction, the OV-rich CuMn2O4/CDs dual-function nanozymes were successfully fabricated, featured by elevated low-valent metal ions (Cu+, Mn2+) and rich OV. The irreversible transfer of metals during the Chan-Lam coupling reaction makes the OV-CuMn2O4/CDs rich pore structure and rough surface. This is conducive to the exposure of active sites and substrate transport, and provides more sites for CDs loading. The presence of Ov and CDs promotes charge transfer and improves the hydrophilicity of CuMn2O4, which makes OV-CuMn2O4/CDs have excellent oxidase-like activity. The reaction mechanism was analyzed by electrochemical experiment, free radical scavenging experiment and EPR test. In terms of the inhibiting effect of D-penicillamine on TMB oxidation and the IFE effect between oxTMB and OV-CuMn2O4/CDs, a colorimetric/fluorescence dual-mode sensor platform was constructed to detect D-PA in water samples and commercial D-penicillamine tablets. Our contribution proposes a new method to construct defect-rich nanozymes and may open up a new avenue for developing dual-mode-based analytical method. In the future, it is considered to further expand the types of etched metals through the Chan-Lam coupling reaction principle to achieve the controlled etching of various metal oxides.

Min Feng: Writing – original draft, Methodology, Investigation, Data curation. Xiaodan Zhang: Writing – review & editing, Supervision. Yuming Huang: Writing – review & editing, Supervision, Methodology, Conceptualization.

ce:conflict-of-interest.ce:para: The authors declare that they have no known competing financial interests or personal relationships that could have appeared to influence the work reported in this paper.
